observations.recommendation:
With the journal entry presented above, the Terminal Leave Benefits Payable
account of the agency was corrected accordingly. In addition, in line with the
observation on Leave Benefits Payable, we recommended and the Management
agreed to require the HR and Accounting Offices to establish a formalized process
where accumulated leave credits are accurately reported and timely recorded in
compliance with IPSAS 39. This includes periodic reconciliations, scheduled
reporting deadlines, and ensuring that the Accounting Office recognizes the full
leave benefits payable at the end of the reporting period.

We recommended and Management agreed, with the Office of the Municipal
Accountant and the Office of the General Services Officer taking the lead,
implement the following to enhance PPE management and financial reporting
accuracy:

a. Expedite Comprehensive PPE Reconciliation and Cleansing. Implement COA
Circular No. 2020-006 to comprehensively resolve all concerns related to PPE
accounts' accuracy, existence, and valuation. This involves conducting
complete physical counts, fully reconciling discrepancies between accounting
records (including property cards and ledger cards) and property unit records,
properly recognizing assets found at stations, and formally disposing of non-
existent or missing PPE items.

b. Establish Effective Asset Management Processes. Implement strong internal
controls for the continuous maintenance of PPE records. This includes
developing and enforcing clear policies for regular reconciliation between
accounting and property records, systematic depreciation calculation, and
seamless communication protocols between the Accounting Department and
the Office of the General Services Officer.

c. Explore integrated software solutions like Digital Asset Management System
(DAMS) that support features like geo-tagging to streamline the recording,
tracking and reporting.

To address these deficiencies and prevent recurrence, we recommended and the
Management agreed to:

a. Strictly enforce compliance with COA Circulars 97-002/2009-002 by ensuring
that only the designated SDOs personally handle all cash disbursements.
Conduct refresher training for SDOs and event coordinators on proper financial
documentation and reporting procedures.

b. Require immediate liquidation of the cash advance and implement a deadline
for submission of liquidation reports. Establish clear consequences for non-
compliance to ensure accountability.

c. Strengthen internal monitoring mechanisms by developing a real-time tracking
system for all cash advances, including automatic notifications for upcoming
liquidation deadlines and early detection of delays. Alternatively, if resources
do not allow for such a system, establish a manual tracking process by
maintaining a centralized cash advance register that is regularly updated,
schedule periodic review meetings to assess the status of all advances, and
assign dedicated personnel to follow up on pending liquidations to ensure
timely corrective action.

d. Establish a mandatory pre-event financial coordination process involving the
SDO, event coordinators, and program heads to clarify roles, responsibilities,
and required documentation before funds are released.

To address the identified deficiencies and mitigate the associated risks, we
recommended and Management agreed to implement the following actions:

a. Develop and Enforce Comprehensive Policies and Procedures:
Draft a detailed Financial Assistance Manual inspired by DSWD’s AICS
program that outlines clear eligibility criteria, tiered assistance rates based on
need and crisis severity, and required documentation (e.g., medical certificates,
death certificates, barangay validation). This manual should also include
standardized assessment tools to objectively evaluate beneficiary needs, and
staff should receive regular training to ensure consistent implementation.
Clear, structured guidelines will eliminate arbitrary decision-making, ensure
consistency, and comply with COA’s mandate for documented controls.

b. Create a Centralized Beneficiary Database:
Implement a digital tracking system to cross-check beneficiaries against
previous claims and verify identities using national IDs or barangay records. A
centralized database minimizes the risk of duplicate claims and fraud.

c. Strengthen Transparency and Accountability Mechanisms:
Publicize eligibility criteria and disbursement details on MGI’s website or
public bulletin boards, establish a Public Assistance Desk for grievances and
appeals, and issue quarterly reports on fund utilization and program impact to
enhance transparency, build public trust, and align with ethical governance
principles.

d. Train Personnel on Policy Adherence:
Conduct regular workshops for LGU staff on applying structured guidelines
consistently, identifying and escalating ethical concerns, and collaborating with
barangay social workers for beneficiary validation to ensure uniform
implementation.

e. Enhance Documentation and Validation:
Require comprehensive case summaries and social assessments by qualified
personnel, such as social workers, to validate beneficiary needs and justify aid
amounts with objective data to create a reliable audit trail and meet best
practice standards for validated support.

f. Implement Post-Disbursement Monitoring:
Conduct random audits of beneficiaries to verify that funds are used for their
intended purposes and to ensure that duplicate claims are not processed.
Ongoing monitoring reinforces accountability and deters misuse of public
funds.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed, through the Municipal
Planning and Development Office, in coordination with Municipal Engineering
Office and other implementing offices concerned, to develop a progressive,
comprehensive and sustainable Development Plan that is financially and technically
doable to ensure optimal utilization of the 20% development fund within the budget
year; regularly monitor the status of utilization and accomplishments of the
implemented Projects/Programs/Activities(PPA’s); and impose liquidated damages
for delayed completion of contracted projects.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer, the Municipal Planning and
Development Officer and other concerned officials, to develop a progressive,
comprehensive and sustainable local disaster PPAs and ensure that the planned
PPAs for disaster risk reduction are necessary, highly doable, attainable and fully
funded during the target implementation period to alleviate the Municipality’s
preparedness for natural calamities, man-made hazards and disasters. Henceforth,
maximize the utilization of the accumulated unexpended balance of DRRMF that
will provide optimum benefit to the constituents, and achieve a disaster resilient
community.

We recommended and the Management agreed to intensify control measures in
the recording of Property Plant and Equipment (PPE), to produce accurate and
reliable presentation of PPE accounts in the financial statements as of December
31, 2023, and specifically addressing the following:

a.1 Require the accounting office to record semi-expendable items noted in the
respective inventory/expense accounts and eliminating the same from the PPE
balances, and the corresponding accumulated depreciation; and
a.2 Issue the corresponding Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) to all
recipients/users of the semi- expendable items.

We recommended and the Management agreed, through the Municipal
Accountant, to intensify the reconciliation its PPE records and compute the
depreciation expenses to record the same in the books. In cases of PPE with no
existing property records, instruct the Municipal Accountant and the LGU
Appraisal Committee to coordinate with one another and come up with the list of
PPEs so that the latter could appraise and determine the fair market value and
estimated useful life of the said properties.

In order to eliminate or lessen the occurrence of checks becoming stale for a
period of time, we recommended and the Management agreed through the
Municipal Treasurer to:
• Send written notices to the payees to verify the existence of the stale checks
and issue a replacement check to payee/s upon request and submission of the
stale check and reasonable explanation why the check became stale. Moreover,
written notice to the payee at least one month before a check becomes stale may
serve as reminder; and
• Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the necessary adjustment/s in the
books of accounts, if any.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that
timely and accurate RPT assessments are made through
managing the factors affecting the availability of updated real
property valuations by initiating, where practicable through
its Sanggunian, the periodic revision of fair market values of
real properties within the Municipality; immediately
implementing an enacted SFMV; and adequately
backstopping such prompt implementation of SFMV with
adequate manpower and prompt compliance to precursor
processes.

We recommended and Management agreed, with the Office of
the Municipal Treasurer as lead, to enhance its operating
practices to further reinforce controls over its RPT and SEF
collections through institutionalizing measures that would
ensure that RPT and SEF collections are seasonably remitted
by collectors, and immediately deposited to an AGDB.

We recommended and Management agreed, with the Offices
of the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer as
leads, to enhance the reliability of its financial representation
by instituting arrangements to ensure that RPT/SEF
receivables and collections are appropriately recognized and
recorded.

We recommended and Management agreed, with the Offices
of the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Treasurer, and Municipal
Accountant as leads, to improve its documents archival system
by instituting mechanisms to bolster record keeping of
government documents and records.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the
Municipal Accounting Office, to reconcile the reported book
balance of loans to MDFO as against the provided outstanding
loan balance as of reporting date and make the necessary
adjusting entry thereof when warranted.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the
Municipal GAD FPS-TWG with the GAD Focal Person as
lead, to enhance the quality and delivery of its GAD initiatives
by adequately and appropriately establishing, maintaining,
and keeping up-to-date its GAD Database through the
implementation and utilization of the Community-Based
Monitoring System and other practicable data gathering
processes/tools.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Accounting Office maintain a
separate special account in the General Fund for the economic
enterprises. Likewise, prepare separate trial balances and
financial statements reflecting the financial condition and
results of their respective operations, in accordance with
existing regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that timely and accurate
RPT assessments are made through managing the factors affecting the availability of
updated real property valuations by initiating, where practicable through its
Sanggunian, the periodic revision of fair market values of real properties within the
Municipality; immediately implementing an enacted SFMV; and adequately
backstopping such prompt implementation of SFMV with adequate manpower and
prompt compliance to precursor processes.

We recommended and Management agreed, with the Office of the Municipal
Treasurer as lead, to enhance its operating practices to further reinforce controls over
its RPT and SEF collections through institutionalizing measures that would ensure that
RPT and SEF collections are seasonably remitted by collectors, and immediately
deposited to an AGDB.

We recommended and Management agreed, with the Offices of the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer as leads, to enhance the reliability of its
financial representation by instituting arrangements to ensure that RPT/SEF
receivables and collections are appropriately recognized and recorded.

We recommended and Management agreed, with the Offices of the Municipal
Mayor, Municipal Treasurer, and Municipal Accountant as leads, to improve its
documents archival system by instituting mechanisms to bolster record keeping of
government documents and records.

In view of the above discussions, we recommended and Management agreed to
correct the noted errors. Accordingly, Management effected the following proposed
adjusting journal entries (PAJEs):

Accounts Debit Credit
Prior Period Adjustment (PPA) 762,012.50
Prepaid Interest 583,500.61
Interest Payable 276,059.31
Interest Expense 1,069,453.80
To correct the error in recording interest expenses incurred in CY 2021and CY 2023 as CY 2022
expenses.
Accounts Debit Credit
Cash in Bank-LCCA 1,250,000.00
Loans Payable - Current 1,250,000.00
To correct the error made in recording loan principal amortization due during the 1st quarter of CY
2023 as CY 2022 loan repayment.
Accounts Debit Credit
Loans Payable – Non-Current 18,775,871.81
Loans Payable – Current 18,775,871.81
To reclassify the current portion of the domestic long-term loan payable.

On other matters discussed, we recommended and Management agreed, with the
Office of the Municipal Accountant as lead, to reconcile the reported book balance of
loans to MDFO as against the provided outstanding loan balance as of reporting date
and make the necessary adjusting entry thereof when warranted.

We recommended and Management agreed, through its BAC, to further improve the quality of its procurement processes to ensure transparency and competitiveness in government procurement by observing, as far as practicable, the required activities in the conduct of competitive bidding as outlined in the Revised IRR of RA 9184; and submitting copies of the perfected contracts and other required supporting documents within the prescribed period to facilitate timely review and evaluation.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the Municipal Accounting Office, to observe proper compliance with the recording processes, taking into consideration the applicable standards and laws, in order to reflect the correct balances of each account.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the Municipal GAD FPS-TWG with the GAD Focal Person as lead, to enhance the quality and delivery of its GAD initiatives by:
a. adequately and appropriately establishing, maintaining, and keeping up-to-date its GAD Database through the implementation and utilization of the Community-Based Monitoring System and other practicable data gathering processes/tools; and

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed, through the Municipal GAD FPS-TWG with the GAD Focal Person as lead, to enhance the quality and delivery of its GAD initiatives by:
b. improving institutional knowledgebase through capacity building of GAD actors on nuances/matters/principles/technicalities such as, but not limited to, risk-base management, result-based management, project management, and gender-issue identification that affect GBP planning, implementation, monitoring, evaluation, and reporting.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the Municipal Accountant, and with the appropriate intervention and support from the LCE, to adopt necessary and practicable measures to exact barangay compliance with the submission of financial documents and related financial reports in order to bolster transparency and accountability.

We recommended and Management agreed through the:
1. Inventory Committee, to regularly perform their designated functions in accordance with what has been mandated of them by Executive Order No. 02, Series of 2019 of the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed through the:
2. Municipal Accounting Office, to:
a. fast track the retrieval of the documents relative to the details of PPEs acquired in prior years. If found to be not available, adopt an alternative measure to ascertain the acquisition details and whereabouts of such PPE item; and

We recommended and Management agreed through the:
2. Municipal Accounting Office, to:
b. in relation to recommendation No. 1, if the documents are already available, prepare the corresponding SLs to serve as a starting point for the perceived municipal wide correction and adjustment of PPEs.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the MENRO to evaluate the reliability of the perimeter enclosures and assess whether or not a new and much stable fence should be constructed in order to discourage easy access both by animals and the people in the area.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the LSB, to improve the processes of planning, prioritization and budgeting of SEF through:
a. the formulation of development programs on education for the Municipal Government, articulating the collective vision of the major stakeholders;

We recommended and Management agreed, through the LSB, to improve the processes of planning, prioritization and budgeting of SEF through:
b. strictly requiring the submission of the respective schools’ budget proposals together with their annual appropriations from the national government to be able to properly identify the items for funding augmentation;

We recommended and Management agreed, through the LSB, to improve the processes of planning, prioritization and budgeting of SEF through:
c. improve the payment process through adoption of a more efficient flow of documents. Also, if necessary, adopt measures as regards to the notification issues whenever a problem arises or a deficiency, if any, will be noted while the documents are on the process for approval; and

We recommended and Management agreed, through the LSB, to improve the processes of planning, prioritization and budgeting of SEF through:
d. monitor on a periodic basis the status of implementation of SEF PPAs, by conducting regular LSB meetings to address the issues and concerns affecting the proper and timely implementation thereof.

1. The General Services Officer together with the Municipal Engineer must conduct an inventory of local roads under the LGU’s jurisdiction. Provide a copy of the inventory reports to the accountant for proper accounting and reconciliation.

2. The Municipal Accountant shall reconcile the inventory report with those recorded in the registry of public infrastructure. Also, the accountant should strictly follow the accounting and reporting procedures provided under COA Circular No. 2015-008 on the valuation of these road asset networks.

We recommended that the Accounting Office maintain a separate special account in the General Fund for the economic enterprises. Likewise, prepare separate trial balances and financial statements reflecting the financial condition and results of their respective operations, in accordance with existing regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed, through its BAC, to further improve the
quality of its procurement processes to ensure transparency and competitiveness in
government procurement by observing, as far as practicable, the required activities in
the conduct of competitive bidding as outlined in the Revised IRR of RA 9184; and
submitting copies of the perfected contracts and other required supporting documents
within the prescribed period to facilitate timely review and evaluation.

On other matters discussed, we recommended and Management agreed, through
the Municipal Accounting Office, to observe proper compliance with the recording
processes, taking into consideration the applicable standards and laws, in order to reflect
the correct balances of each account.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the Municipal GAD FPS-
TWG with the GAD Focal Person as lead, to enhance the quality and delivery of its GAD
initiatives by:

a. adequately and appropriately establishing, maintaining, and keeping up-to-
date its GAD Database through the implementation and utilization of the
Community-Based Monitoring System and other practicable data gathering
processes/tools; and
b. improving institutional knowledgebase through capacity building of GAD
actors on nuances/matters/principles/technicalities such as, but not limited
to, risk-base management, result-based management, project management, and
gender-issue identification that affect GBP planning, implementation,
monitoring, evaluation, and reporting.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the Municipal Accountant,
and with the appropriate intervention and support from the LCE, to adopt necessary and
practicable measures to exact barangay compliance with the submission of financial
documents and related financial reports in order to bolster transparency and
accountability.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LGUs of the province further enhance the quality of its individual LDRRM management processes by taking concrete actions to address the issues related to manpower, data and information, and formalization to reinforce the establishment of an enabling level of coordination in a collective setting and ensure disaster resiliency.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the MDRRMO, to:

a. revisit the guidelines relative to the utilization of the LDRRMF to ensure and enforce the strict compliance thereof;
b. review and assess the projects, programs and activities with respect to their compliance with existing laws, rules and regulations as a requirement before these undertakings are endeavored to form part of the LDRRMFIP; and
c. identify projects, programs and activities, with a view of the funding constraints, by assessing which to prioritize and implement vis-à-vis the LGU determined disaster related issues;

We recommended and Management agreed, through the MDRRMO, to review the guidelines in updating the LDRRMFIP to ensure that relevant measures are properly observed. Likewise, evaluate the compliance of the LGU as regards the realignment procedures to warrant that the legislation enacted is sufficient and appropriate with what has been required by the law.

We further recommended and Management agreed, through the Municipal Accountant, to:
a. strengthen the controls being adopted pertaining to the proper identification and recording of transactions by diligently reviewing the nature and purpose of each transaction in order to reflect their true and correct balances; and
b. if warranted, draw necessary adjusting journal entries, in view of the preceding discussions and/or presentations in order to facilitate the proper classification of transactions to their proper accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed through the:
1. Municipal Development Council to, observe the provisional guidelines in the preparation of the annual and supplemental investment programs; and
2. Sangguniang Bayan, to comply with the existing provisions as regards the realignment procedures to ensure that legislation enacted is sufficient and appropriate in accordance with the provisions of the law.
3. Municipal Development Council and Municipal Engineering Office, to:
a. re-evaluate the status of the Municipal Government’s programs, projects and activities under 20% DF. Ascertain the constraints that were seen to have deterred the supposed timely implementation thereof. Formally document the identified impediments and/or limitations and have them timely updated as ready reference for future undertakings; and
b. strengthen the monitoring process with regard to the project execution in order to identify the possible hindrances that may impede the implementation so that appropriate actions may be undertaken at the earliest possible time to address the issue.
4. Municipal Engineering Office, to improve their reporting practices through adoption of mechanisms that will address the issues on report completeness. Also, employ necessary control such as the reconciliation of relevant data with the other departments within the municipality to avoid inconsistencies.

We recommended and Management agreed through the:
1. Inventory Committee, to regularly perform their designated functions in accordance with what has been mandated of them by Executive Order No. 02, Series of 2019 of the Municipality.
2. Municipal GSO, to:
a. review all the AREs issued pertaining to the semi-expandable items and other misclassified accounts, and if warranted, change the issued ARE with the appropriate form such as the ICS;
b. the GSO, together with municipal accounting office, should conduct an assessment and review of all procured properties in order to arrive into one common ground with regard to the treatment of every items for easy reconciliation;
c. strictly adhere to the guidelines set by the Handbook on Property and Supply Management and other related issuances on sound practices, such as but not limited to setting an effective internal control measures over PPEs; and
d. prepare the updated Inspection and Inventory Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) and facilitate the proper disposal procedures in collaboration with the Inventory, Appraisal and Disposal Committee as mandated by Executive Order No. 02, Series of 2019 in accordance with the existing rules and regulations.
3. Municipal Accounting Office, to:
a. fast track the retrieval of the documents relative to the details of PPEs acquired in prior years. If found to be not available, adopt an alternative measure to ascertain the acquisition details and whereabouts of such PPE item; and
b. in relation to recommendation No. 1, if the documents are already available, prepare the corresponding SLs to serve as a starting point for the perceived municipal wide correction and adjustment of PPEs.

observations.reason: The schools were not able to submit their appropriations from the national government, hence, items for funding were based only on School Improvement Plan and Annual Investment Plan.

observations.recommendation:

1. Municipal Accountant to adopt the perpetual inventory method of accounting for all supplies and materials procured. Also, maintain subsidiary ledger cards for each item of inventory;
2. General Services Officer to (i) maintain stock cards for supplies and materials; and
3. Management to exert effort to provide central storage room to store and safe keep all procured supplies and materials.

1. The General Services Officer together with the Municipal Engineer must conduct an inventory of local roads under the LGU’s jurisdiction. Provide a copy of the inventory reports to the accountant for proper accounting and reconciliation.

3. The Local Chief Executive must monitor the compliance of the concerned officials to the above-mentioned circular.

We recommended that the Accounting Office maintain a separate special account in the General Fund for the economic enterprises. activities under 20% DF. Ascertain the constraints that were seen to have
deterred the supposed timely implementation thereof. Formally document the
identified impediments and/or limitations and have them timely updated as
ready reference for future undertakings; and
b. strengthen the monitoring process with regard to the project execution in
order to identify the possible hindrances that may impede the implementation
so that appropriate actions may be undertaken at the earliest possible time to
address the issue.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the Municipal Engineering
Office, to improve their reporting practices through adoption of mechanisms that will
address the issues on report completeness. Also, employ necessary control such as the
reconciliation of relevant data with the other departments within the municipality to avoid
inconsistencies.

We recommended and Management agreed through the:
1. Inventory Committee, to regularly perform their designated functions in
accordance with what has been mandated of them by Executive Order No. 02,
Series of 2019 of the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the:
1. Municipal GSO, to:
a. review all the AREs issued pertaining to the semi-expandable items and other
misclassified accounts, and if warranted, change the issued ARE with the
appropriate form such as the ICS;
b. the GSO, together with municipal accounting office, should conduct an
assessment and review of all procured properties in order to arrive into one
common ground with regard to the treatment of every items for easy
reconciliation;
c. strictly adhere to the guidelines set by the Handbook on Property and Supply
Management and other related issuances on sound practices, such as but not
limited to setting an effective internal control measures over PPEs; and
d. prepare the updated Inspection and Inventory Report of Unserviceable
Properties (IIRUP) and facilitate the proper disposal procedures in
collaboration with the Inventory, Appraisal and Disposal Committee as
mandated by Executive Order No. 02, Series of 2019 in accordance with the
existing rules and regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the General Services
Office, to prepare the updated Inspection and Inventory Report of Unserviceable
Properties (IIRUP) and facilitate the proper disposal procedures in collaboration
with the Inventory, Appraisal and Disposal Committee as mandated by Executive
Order No. 02, Series of 2019 in accordance with the existing rules and regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the Municipal
Accounting Office, to:
1. fast track the retrieval of the documents relative to the details of PPEs
acquired in prior years. If found to be not available, adopt an alternative
measure to ascertain the acquisition details and whereabouts of such PPE
item; and
2. in relation to recommendation No. 1, if the documents are already
available, prepare the corresponding SLs to serve as a starting point for the
perceived municipal wide correction and adjustment of PPEs.

We recommended that the LGUs of the province collectively and individually ensure the effective and efficient delivery of LDRRM responsibilities by enhancing the level of coordination through managing the

observations.recommendation: We recommended the: 1. Municipal Accountant to adopt the perpetual inventory method of accounting for all supplies and materials procured. Also, maintain subsidiary ledger cards for each item of inventory; 2. Management to exert effort to provide central storage room to store and safe keep all procured supplies and materials.

observations.reason: Per the municipality: stock cards were not maintained because supplies will have zero balance since all supplies were released immediately after delivery. Bidding for construction of new municipal building is already ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Council formulate a detailed Annual Investment Program, incorporating therein the complete description, exact location, estimated cost, and timeframe for evaluation of their completion of each program, project and activity to facilitate evaluation of its completion, appropriation, utilization and implementation, pursuant to existing laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended the following: 1. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory taking of all properties owned by the Municipal Government to determine the accuracy, reliability and integrity of the PPE account balances at year-end; and 2. Initiate the immediate disposal of the unserviceable assets in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445 to avoid further deterioration.
observations.reason: Subsumed in the new audit observation as discussed in Part II (Observation No. 4-PPE) of this report.

observations.recommendation: 1. The General Services Officer together with the Municipal Engineer must conduct an inventory of local roads under the LGU’s jurisdiction. Provide a copy of the inventory reports to the accountant for proper accounting and reconciliation. 2. The Municipal Accountant shall reconcile the inventory report with those recorded in the registry of public infrastructure. Also, the accountant should strictly follow the accounting and reporting procedures provided under COA Circular No. 2015-008 on the valuation of these road asset networks. 3. The Local Chief Executive must monitor the compliance of the concerned officials to the above-mentioned circular.

observations.reason: Accounts and Position are very difficult and time consuming to identify, recompute and retrieve.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting Office maintain a separate special account in the General Fund for the economic enterprises. Likewise, prepare separate trial balances and financial statements reflecting the financial condition and results of their respective operations, in accordance with existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016
observations.reason: Accounts and Position are very difficult and time consuming to identify, recompute and retrieve.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LGUs of the province collectively and individually
ensure the effective and efficient delivery of LDRRM responsibilities by enhancing
the level of coordination through managing the challenges on the dependencies that
affect the essential management processes.

We recommended that the LGUs of the province ensure the effective and
efficient execution of disaster responses through the establishment of appropriately
contextualized disaster protocols.

We recommended and the Management agreed, through the MENRO, to:
1. review the Programs under each strategy for viability, and if warranted,
revise the plan to ensure doability and sustainability, taking into
consideration the possible hindrances on non-implementation. In
addition, check the budget allocation for each program to ensure that
enough resources are given to every program;
2. revisit, evaluate, and assess the Municipal Government’s presently
adopted SWMP and have the same aligned with that of the one approved
by the authority;
3. closely monitor the processes and protocols being adopted by the six (6)
barangays allowed by the MENRO to collect, transport and dump their
solid waste in the landfill to ensure that proper procedures thereto are
being followed; and
4. exert extra effort in monitoring the Municipal Government compliance
with Laws, Rules and Regulations with regards to Solid Waste
Management.

We recommended and the Management agreed, through the MENRO, to:
1. regularly monitor the usage and availability of personnel protective gears,
and if needed, consider purchasing new ones to ensure the safety of each
personnel involved in waste management. Additionally, as a control
measure, encourage each personnel to wear the necessary protective gears
and equipment in line with their duties, and provide appropriate
disciplinary actions to those who failed to wear one;
2. evaluate the reliability of the perimeter enclosures and assess whether or
not a new and much stable fence should be constructed in order to
discourage easy access both by animals and the people in the area;
3. strictly limit the access of any individual in the landfill premises by
requiring each visitor to log in their information for monitoring purposes;
and
4. consider drafting a policy and/or guideline on the wearing of protective
gears and equipment for visitors, wastes pickers/scavengers and personnel
whenever they are within the vicinity.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the LSB, to:
1. improve the processes of planning, prioritization and budgeting of SEF
through:
a. timely preparation and authorization of the SEF Budget in order for the SEF
PPAs to be included in the Municipal Government’s Annual Investment
plan, as well as to avoid undue delay in the implementation thereof;
b. instituting measures to make the LSB more proactive and effective in
introducing educational reforms within its capacity;
c. the formulation of development programs on education for the Municipal
Government, articulating the collective vision of the major stakeholders;
d. increasing participation and involvement of various sectors, including but
not limited to parents, teachers, non-teaching personnel, and among others,
in the regular consultative meetings of the LSB;
e. strictly requiring the submission of the respective schools’ budget proposals
together with their annual appropriations from the national government to
be able to properly identify the items for funding augmentation;
f. creation of an ad hoc Technical Working Group (TWG) to spearhead the
evaluation and prioritization of the submitted proposals, specifically the
viability of the each planned PPAs; and
g. strict observance of the requirement set forth by the law, specifically
Section 7 (b) of the Republic Act No. 10410 by allocating adequate funds
for the implementation of the ECCD Programs.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the LSB, to:
1. improve the payment process through adoption of a more efficient flow of
documents. Also, if necessary, adopt measures as regards to the notification
issues whenever a problem arises or a deficiency, if any, will be noted while
the documents are on the process for approval;
2. consider the early preparation and approval of the SEF budget in
adherence to the timeline and procedures on budget preparation of the
Municipality, to avoid eventual delays in the implementation of PPAs; and
3. monitor on a periodic basis the status of implementation of SEF PPAs, by
conducting regular LSB meetings to address the issues and concerns
affecting the proper and timely implementation thereof.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the LSB and the
Municipal Planning Development Office (MPDO), hasten the development of
measures, within its capacity, to assess projects, activities and programs with respect
not only to the desired outputs, but as well to the outcomes and impacts these projects
have to the locality.

We recommended that chief
management executive cause
the creation of a
operations manual, evolve a manual of
policies and procedures with
reference to existing COA
policies and procedures on cash
management system to address the above cited weaknesses in internal control.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018
AAR 2018

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the:

2. Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to record the RPT and SET based on the certified list from the Treasury Office.

The Municipal Accountant shall reconcile the inventory report with those recorded in the registry of public infrastructure. The Local Chief Executive must monitor the compliance of the concerned officials to the above mentioned circular.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management adopt the Perpetual Inventory System in handling its supplies inventory. Further, require the Inventory Team to conduct complete physical inventory at least every six months, as of June 30 and December 31, each year, and submit results thereon in a prescribed form in order to ascertain the correctness of the inventory balance and early detection of possible losses.
observations.reason: Management find it very difficult to apply Perpetual Inventory Method. No internal control installed yet in management of supplies and inventory due to absence of GSO stockroom.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that chief executive cause the creation of a task force to
evolve a manual of policies and procedures with reference to existing COA
policies and procedures on cash management system to address the above
cited weaknesses in internal control.

In order to address the observations and increase collections, we
recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
1. Improve revenue forecasting by using the forecasting tools provided
by the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF);
2. Inspect business establishments within the jurisdiction of the
Municipality to ensure effective and efficient collection of business taxes
as mandated under [Section 470 (d)(4)] of LGC;
3. Maintain and update the tax information system of the Municipality
thru:
establishing a tax roll for each type of tax and revenue imposed in
the Revenue Code;
conduct of tax census and develop revenue data bank; and
maintaining a systematic and organized records of payments of all
taxpayers.
4. Furnish the Accountant the complete and accurate list of taxpayers with
the corresponding amount of taxes due and collectibles for the year as
basis to record the correct Real Property Tax (RPT) and Special
Education Tax (SET) Receivables in the books; and
5. Institute measures and strategies to efficiently collect various revenues
provided under the Revenue Code of the Municipality, such as:
sending of tax/revenue bills to taxpayers;
thorough monitoring of payment of taxpayers and identification
and collection of tax delinquencies; and
Strict implementation of sanctions and penalties on erring
taxpayers.
6. Correct the coding of revenue accounts to align and conform to the
prescribed PPSAS Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the:
1. Municipal Treasurer to prepare and furnish the Accounting Office with
the Certified List of Taxpayers and tax due and collectible every
beginning of the year; and
2. Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to record the RPT and SET
Receivables based on the certified list from the Treasury Office.

We recommended the:
1. Municipal Accountant to adopt the perpetual inventory method of
accounting for all supplies and materials procured. Also, maintain
subsidiary ledger cards for each item of inventory;
2. General Services Officer to (i) maintain stock cards and property cards
for supplies and materials; (ii) prepare and submit monthly the
Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued to the Accounting Office
for the recording of issuances; and
3. Management to exert effort to provide central storage room to store
and safe keep all procured supplies and materials.

We recommended that the Local School Board:

a. Confine its projects, programs and activities to those expressly enumerated in Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and Section 4 of DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017, in the allocation and utilization of the Special Education Fund; and

b. Stop the practice of utilizing the Special Education Fund to expenditures other than those expressly provided by laws, rules and regulation, in order to avoid suspensions/disallowances in audit and attain the purposes for which the fund was established and appropriated.

a. We recommended that the MDRRMC decide on the use of the unexpended balance and incorporate the same in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan.

b. We recommended that the MDRRMC not stop the practice of charging related to disaster expenditures against the LDRRMF for management purposes not related to disaster risk reduction activities pursuant to RA 10121 and JMC No. 2013-1 of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG to avoid suspensions in audit.

c. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepares and submit through the MDRRMO the monthly Reports and Utilization of the LDRRMF.

a. We recommended that the management should direct the Municipal Planning and Development Council formulate a detailed Annual Investment Program, incorporating therein the complete description, exact location, estimated cost, and timeframe for completion of each program, project and activity to facilitate effective evaluation of their implementation, appropriation, utilization and implementation, pursuant to existing laws, rules and regulations.

b. We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Council and the implementing departments concerned maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund so that all of its programmed projects could be implemented on time to ensure that intended beneficiaries can avail of the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom.

We recommended that the Municipality establish LCPC and set aside one percent (1%) of the IRA for the strengthening and implementation of the LCPC programs in compliance with the provisions of RA 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.

We recommended the following:

a. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal GSO to maintain the PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively for each item as control records and to facilitate the regular reconciliation of records. This should be given utmost importance to determine the accuracy and reliability of the PPE account balances at year-end;

b. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of all properties owned by the Municipal Government to determine the accuracy, reliability and integrity of the PPE account balances at year-end; and

c. Initiate the immediate disposal of the unserviceable assets in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445 to avoid further deterioration.
observations.reason: Retrieval of old manual documents (1) records were lost/destroyed or unreadable.

observations.recommendation:
a. The General Services Officer together with the Municipal Engineer must conduct an inventory of local roads under the LGU’s jurisdiction. Provide a copy of the inventory reports to the accountant for proper accounting and reconciliation.

b. The municipal accountant shall reconcile the inventory report with those recorded in the registry of public infrastructure. Also, the accountant should strictly follow the accounting and reporting procedures provided under COA Circular No. 2015-008 on the valuation of these road asset networks.

c. The local chief executive must monitor the compliance of the concerned officials to the above mentioned circular.

We recommended that the Accounting Office maintain a separate special account in the General Fund for the Economic Enterprises. Likewise, prepare separate trial balances and financial statements reflecting the financial condition and results of operations of the Economic Enterprises. Further, initialize formulation of Financial Statements.
observations.reason: Old manual records were lost/destroyed or unreadable.

observations.recommendation:
a. The management should direct the officials concerned to ensure that the 20% of the IRA is optimally utilized in the prompt implementation of programs and projects to achieve the desirable socio-economic development and as prescribed under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-01.

b. Further, management should enhance the full rate of implementation of projects funded from 20% Development Fund so that the desired benefits and services which go with the projects are delivered to the constituents of the municipality without delay.

c. Furthermore, Management should improve the project monitoring and evaluation system which has the potential of mitigating delays in project implementation arising from those mentioned factors.

We advised the management to conduct a complete physical inventory of all assets owned by the Municipal Government. Further, identify unserviceable properties and record the same at the same time in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) to facilitate the disposal of these assets, and eventual dropping from the books.
observations.reason: The agency failed to conduct a complete physical inventory of its property, plant and equipment.

observations.recommendation:
a. The General Services Officer together with the Municipal Engineer must conduct an inventory of local roads under the LGU’s jurisdiction. Provide a copy of the inventory reports to the accountant for proper accounting and reconciliation.
b. The municipal road asset accountant shall reconcile the inventory report with those recorded in the registry of public infrastructure. Also, the accountant should strictly follow the accounting and reporting procedures provided under COA Circular No. 2015-008 on the valuation of these road asset networks.
c. The local chief executive must monitor the compliance of the concerned officials to the above mentioned circular.
observations.managementAction: Issuance of memorandum order to conduct inventory and provide copy of inventory reports to the accountant; Municipal Engineer to conduct joint inspection and inventory of the road asset networks in the 17 barangays of the municipality.
observations.reason: On-going retrieval of documents

observations.recommendation:
a. The management should direct the officials concerned to ensure that the 20% of the IRA is optimally utilized in the prompt implementation of programs and projects to achieve the desirable socio-economic development and as prescribed under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-01.
b. Further, management should enhance the full rate of implementation of projects funded from 20% Development Fund so that the desired benefits and services which go with the projects are delivered to the constituents of the municipality without delay.
c. Furthermore, Management should improve the project monitoring and evaluation system which has the potential of mitigating delays in project implementation arising from those mentioned factors.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Findings No. 6 due to significant amount of unexpended fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management adopt the Perpetual Inventory Method. Further, require the Inventory Team to undertake complete physical inventory at least every six months, as of June 30 and December 31, each year, and submit results thereon in a prescribed form in order to ascertain the correctness of the inventory balance and early detection of possible losses.

Further, identify unserviceable properties and record at the same time in Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property to facilitate the disposal and eventual dropping from the books. While these unserviceable properties are not yet disposed, record them under Other Asset account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local School Board:
1. Confine its projects, programs and activities to those expressly
enumerated in Section 272 of RA 7160 and Section 4 of DepEd,
DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017, in the allocation and
utilization of the Special Education Fund; and
2. Stop the practice of utilizing the Special Education Fund to
expenditures other than those expressly provided by laws, rules and
regulation, in order to avoid suspensions/disallowances in audit and
attain the purposes for which the fund was established and
appropriated.

We recommended that the MDRRMC decide on the use of the
unexpended balance and incorporate the same in the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan.

We recommended that the MDRRMC stop the practice of charging
expenditures against the LDRRMF for purposes not related to disaster risk
reduction management activities pursuant to RA 10121 and JMC No. 2013-1 of
the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG to avoid suspensions in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepares and submit
through the MDRRMO the monthly Reports and Utilization of the LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Council
formulate a detailed Annual Investment Program, incorporating therein the
complete description, exact location, estimated cost, and timeframe for
completion of each program, project and activity to facilitate evaluation of its
appropriation, utilization and implementation, pursuant to existing laws, rules
and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Council
and the implementing departments concerned maximize the utilization of the 20%
Development Fund so that all of its programmed development projects could be
implemented on time to ensure that intended beneficiaries can avail of the
maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom.

We recommended that the Municipality establish LCPC and allocate one
percent (1%) of its IRA for the strengthening and implementation of the LCPC
programs in compliance with the provisions of RA 9344 and DILG Memorandum
Circular No. 2012-120.

We recommended the following:
1. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal GSO to maintain the
PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively for each item as
control records and to facilitate the regular reconciliation of records. This
should be given utmost importance to determine the accuracy and
reliability of the PPE account balances at year-end;
2. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical
inventory taking of all properties owned by the Municipal Government to
determine the accuracy, reliability and integrity of the PPE account
balances at year-end; and
3. Initiate the immediate disposal of the unserviceable assets in accordance
with Section 79 of PD 1445 to avoid further deterioration.

a. The Bids and Awards Committee and other concerned Municipal officials should strictly adhere to the provisions of Executive Order No. 49 s. 1993 and Department of Health Administrative Order No. 2012-0023 on the procurement of essential medicines that will be used in the RHU.
b. Drugs and medicines not in the PNF Manual should no longer be included in the procurement. But if the concerned Municipal Health Officer (MHO) wish to include those medicines on the list, a request must be done based on the guidelines for the inclusion of the items.

a. The Local Chief Executive shall prioritize the establishment of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office.
b. The Local Sanggunian must facilitate the creation of the DRRM positions intended for the LDRRMO.
c. Strict observance of the provisions of NDRRMC, DILG, DBM, and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1.

a. The municipal budget officer shall monitor the expenditures of the municipality in order not to exceed the approved budget.
b. The municipal accountant shall see to it that expenditures are charged to its proper account.
c. All municipal officials concerned must strictly follow the provisions of RA 7160 and other existing Rules and Regulations in preparing the Annual Budget.

We recommended that the Accounting Office maintain a separate special account in the General Fund for the economic enterprises. Likewise, prepare separate trial balances and financial statements reflecting the financial condition and results of operations, in accordance with existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: Retrieval of old documents from OMA, MTO and MO to classify assets belonging to Economic Enterprises to initialize formulation of Financial Statements.
observations.reason: Old manual records from OMA, MTO and MO were lost/destroyed or unreadable.

observations.recommendation:
a. Prior to approval of loans, the HR officer should prepare the pay slip of the employee which will aid the approving authority in determining whether such loan should be approved or not.
b. Comply with the general provisions of the GAA on the minimum allowed net take home pay of an employee.

The management should exert diligent and continuous efforts to have a full accounting, settlement and monitoring of outstanding cash advances by requiring all accountable officers concerned to refund, submit documents and/or make a proper accounting of their outstanding cash advances, and establish a policy on monitoring of cash advances to ensure that immediate action is taken to prevent recurrence of unliquidated cash advances.

Management should provide appropriation for the Insurance coverage of properties for the protection of properties, buildings, and office equipment in accordance with Sec. 5 of RA 656, Section 489, Volume I of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual, and PD 245.

1. Confirm the amount of ₱2,422,162.02 from the list of the creditors including the employees whether the said payables are still valid and bonafide obligation of the agency and make necessary adjustments in the books of accounts if proven to be without existing creditors for an accurate presentation of the accounts payable in the Financial Statements.
2. Further, exert effort to trace the history of the payable accounts by retrieving the previous documents to identify which amount should be dropped from the books.

The management was advised to submit the above mentioned financial reports/accounts that have reached their due dates. Further, we recommend to treasury, accounting and other concerned offices to adhere with prescribed time frame of submission of accounts as mandated under Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and Section 7 of COA Circular No. 94-001.

We suggested for the management to follow the minimum requirements provided under Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 (RA 9003) in operating its sanitary landfill. Further, conduct information drive/awareness within the municipality regarding the importance of proper segregation of solid wastes.

We recommend that management adopt the Perpetual Inventory System in handling its supplies inventory. Further, require the Inventory Team to undertake complete physical inventory at least every six months, as of June 30 and December 31, each year, and submit results thereon in a prescribed form in order to ascertain the correctness of the inventory balance and early detection of possible losses.

observations.reason: No available warehouse or storage facility yet.

observations.recommendation: Management should provide appropriation for the Insurance coverage of properties for the protection of properties, buildings, and office equipment in accordance with Sec. 5 of RA 656, Section 489, Volume I of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual, and PD 245.
observations.managementAction: Provide appropriation for the insurance of municipal properties.
observations.reason: The municipal treasurer already applied for insurance in GSIS. However, Employees from GSIS have not returned yet to ascertain the list of properties that should be insured.

Further, exert effort to trace the history of the payable accounts by retrieving the previous documents to identify which amount should be dropped from the books.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation

We therefore recommended the following:


a. The Bids and Awards Committee and other concerned Municipal  officials
   should strictly adhere to the provisions of Executive Order No. 49  s.
   1993 and Department of Health Administrative Order  No.  2012-0023  on
   the procurement of essential medicines that will be used in the RHU.


b. Drugs and medicines not in  the  PNF  Manual  should  no  longer  be
   included in the procurement. But if the concerned  Municipal  Health
   Officer (MHO) wish to include those medicines on the list, a request
   must be done based on the guidelines for the inclusion of the items.

Recommendation

We therefore recommended the following:


a. The Local Chief Executive shall prioritize the  establishment  of  the
   Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office.


b. The  Local  Sanggunian  must  facilitate  the  creation  of  the  DRRM
   positions intended for the LDRRMO.


c. Strict observance of the provisions of NDRRMC, DILG,  DBM,  and  CSC
   Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1

Recommendation

We therefore recommended the following:


a. The General Services Officer together with the Municipal Engineer must
   conduct an inventory of local  roads  under  the  LGU’s  jurisdiction.
   Provide a copy of the inventory reports to the accountant  for  proper
   accounting and reconciliation.
b. The municipal accountant shall reconcile  the  inventory  report  with
   those recorded in the registry of  public  infrastructure.  Also,  the
   accountant  should  strictly  follow  the  accounting  and   reporting
   procedures provided under COA Circular No. 2015-008 on  the  valuation
   of these road asset networks.


c. The local  chief  executive  must  monitor  the  compliance  of  the
   concerned officials to the above mentioned circular.

Recommendation

We recommended the following:


a. The municipal budget officer shall monitor  the  expenditures  of  the
   municipality in order not to exceed the approved budget.


b. The municipal accountant shall see to it that expenditures are charged
   to its proper account.


c. All municipal officials concerned must strictly follow the  provisions
   of RA 7160 and other existing Rules and Regulations in  preparing  the
   Annual Budget.

Recommendation

We recommended that the Accounting Office maintain a  separate  special
account in the General Fund for the economic enterprises. Likewise,  prepare
separate trial balances and financial statements  reflecting  the  financial
condition and results of their respective  operations,  in  accordance  with
existing regulations.

Recommendation

We therefore recommended the following:


    a. Prior to approval of loans, the HR officer  should  prepare  the
       pay slip of the employee which will aid the approving  authority
       in determining whether such loan should be approved or not.


    b. Comply with the general provisions of the  GAA  on  the  minimum
       allowed net take home pay of an employee.

Recommendation

We recommended the following:


a. The management should direct the officials concerned  to  ensure  that
   the 20% of the IRA is optimally utilized in the prompt  implementation
   of programs and  projects  to  achieve  the  desirable  socio-economic
   development and as prescribed under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular
   No. 2011-01.


b. Further, management should enhance the full rate of implementation  of
   projects funded from 20% Development Fund so that the desired benefits
   and  services  which  go  with  the  projects  are  delivered  to  the
   constituents of the municipality without delay.


c. Furthermore, Management should improve the project  monitoring  and
   evaluation system which has the potential of mitigating  delays  in
   project implementation arising from those mentioned factors.

Representation Allowances and Transportation Allowances (RATA) of various Municipal officials totaling ₱3,062,850.00 were charged to Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses, thereby understating the Personal Services account.
observations.managementAction: Adjust current year’s financial statements to conform with accounting rules and regulations

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned employees should strictly observe the proper procedures on procurement of food supplies and office supplies.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO shall facilitate the prompt processing of documents prior to purchase of items.

observations.recommendation:
We advise the Municipal Accountant to submit the liquidation reports on cash advances pertaining to preparation for the provincial meet.

Also, we recommend that the concerned municipal officials and local school boards should strictly adhere to the programs and projects recommended under DECS-DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01, 01-A, and 01-B on the utilization of Special Education Fund.
observations.managementAction: Conduct a joint meeting together with the local school boards to discuss matters concerning utilization of SEF.
Review the transactions for proper classification of expenses especially those pertaining to meals and snacks. Also, observe careful preparation of 2017 annual budget.

observations.recommendation: The management should exert diligent and continuous efforts to have a full accounting, settlement and monitoring of outstanding cash advances by requiring all accountable officers concerned to refund, submit documents and/or make a proper accounting of their outstanding cash advances, and establish a policy on monitoring of cash advances to ensure that immediate action is taken to prevent recurrence of unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Require the employees concerned to liquidate immediately their cash advances. Further, require employees to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period under existing Rules and Regulations.
observations.reason: The unliquidated cash advances has been reduced by up to 50%.
